content: Let me start by reading the relevant instruction files and then examine the Lean files I need to audit.
I'll read the instruction files and then audit the specified Lean files.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.

Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.

132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.

137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.

142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.

154	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
155	
156	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
157	
158	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
159	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
160	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
161	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
162	
163	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
164	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
165	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
166	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
167	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
168	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
169	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
170	
171	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
172	
173	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
174	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
175	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
176	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
177	
178	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
179	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
180	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
181	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
182	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
183	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
184	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
185	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
186	
187	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
188	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
189	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
190	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
191	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
192	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
193	law is definitional. -/
194	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
195	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
196	
197	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
198	
199	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
200	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
201	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
202	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
203	
204	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
205	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
206	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
207	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
208	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
209	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
210	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
211	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
212	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
213	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
214	
215	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
216	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
217	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
218	
219	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
220	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
221	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
222	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
223	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
224	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
225	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
226	
227	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
228	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
229	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
230	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
231	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
232	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
233	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
234	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
235	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
236	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
237	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
238	
239	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
240	
241	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
242	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
243	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
244	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
245	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
246	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
247	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
248	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
249	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
250	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
251	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
252	
253	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
254	
255	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
256	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
257	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
258	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
259	
260	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
261	
262	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
263	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
264	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
265	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
266	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
267	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
268	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
269	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
270	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
271	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
272	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
273	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
274	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
275	
276	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
277	sheaf.**
278	
279	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
280	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
281	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
282	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
283	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
284	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
285	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
286	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
287	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
288	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
289	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
290	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
291	
292	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
293	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
294	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
295	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
296	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
297	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
298	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
299	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
300	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
301	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
302	
303	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
304	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
305	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
306	sheafification counit. -/
307	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
308	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
309	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
310	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
311	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
312	
313	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
314	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
315	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
316	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
317	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
318	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
319	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
320	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
321	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
322	
323	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X`.** (Blueprint
324	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
325	For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
326	`(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
327	group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
328	
329	**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
330	hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
331	sectionwise flatness — false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
332	Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `η` the
333	sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
334	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso, giving
335	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
336	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`, `α` the
337	     presheaf-of-modules associator;
338	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso, giving
339	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
340	Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
341	`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (η = `toSheafify ∈ J.W`, and `J.W` is
342	stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
343	functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
344	`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
345	`letI instMS` below. -/
346	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
347	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
348	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
349	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
350	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
351	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
352	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
353	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
354	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
355	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
356	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
357	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
358	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
359	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
360	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
361	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
362	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
363	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
364	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
365	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
366	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
367	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
368	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
369	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
370	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
371	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
372	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
373	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
374	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
375	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
376	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
377	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
378	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
379	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
380	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
381	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
382	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
383	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
384	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
385	  have e2 := a.mapIso
386	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
387	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
388	
389	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
390	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
391	
392	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
393	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
394	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
395	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
396	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
397	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
398	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
399	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
400	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
401	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
402	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
403	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
404	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
405	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
406	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
407	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
408	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
409	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
410	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
411	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
415	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
416	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
417	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
418	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
419	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
420	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
421	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
422	
423	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
424	
425	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
426	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
427	
428	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
429	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
430	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
431	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
432	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
433	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
434	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
435	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
436	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
437	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
438	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
439	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
440	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
441	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
442	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
443	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
444	      definitionally.
445	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
446	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
447	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
448	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
449	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
450	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
451	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
452	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
453	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
454	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
455	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
456	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
457	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
458	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
459	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
460	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
461	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
462	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
463	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
464	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
465	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
466	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
467	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
468	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
469	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
470	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
471	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
472	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
473	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
474	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
475	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
476	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
477	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
478	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
479	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
480	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
481	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
482	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
483	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
484	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
485	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
486	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
487	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
488	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
489	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
490	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
491	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
492	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
493	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
494	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
495	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
496	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
497	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
498	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
499	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
500	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.

67	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
68	
69	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
70	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
71	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
72	
73	namespace PresheafOfModules
74	
75	open InternalHom Opposite
76	
77	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
78	
79	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
80	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
81	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
82	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
83	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
84	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
85	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
86	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
89	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
90	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
91	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
92	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
93	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
94	  exact
95	    { toFun := fun φ =>
96	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
98	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
99	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
100	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
101	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
102	      invFun := fun r =>
103	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
104	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
105	      left_inv := fun φ => by
106	        ext Y
107	        dsimp only
108	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
109	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
110	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
111	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
112	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
113	        rfl
114	      right_inv := fun r => by
115	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
116	            (restr X.unop
117	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
118	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
119	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
120	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
121	        exact mul_one r }
122	
123	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
124	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
125	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
126	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
127	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
128	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
129	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
130	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
131	    (fun {X Y} f => by
132	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
133	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
134	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
135	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
136	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
137	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
138	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
139	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
140	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
141	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
142	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
143	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
144	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
145	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
146	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
147	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
148	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
149	      rw [hrm] at key
150	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
151	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
152	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
153	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
154	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
155	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
156	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
157	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
158	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
159	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
160	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
161	
162	end PresheafOfModules
163	
164	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
165	
166	namespace Scheme
167	
168	namespace Modules
169	
170	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
171	
172	open Opposite in
173	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
174	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
175	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
176	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
177	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
178	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
179	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
180	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
181	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
182	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
183	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
184	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)
185	
186	open Opposite in
187	/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
188	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
189	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
190	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
191	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
192	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
193	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
194	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
195	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
196	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
197	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
198	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
199	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
200	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))
201	
202	open Opposite in
203	/-- **Leg-B (inverse direction): the unit codomain ring-iso swap for `invFun`** `𝟙_Y(W') ⟶
204	restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
205	`ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of
206	`dualUnitRingSwap`.  By `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso` it is an isomorphism and is the inverse of
207	`dualUnitRingSwap f W'` (they cancel by `IsIso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`). -/
208	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
209	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
210	    (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
211	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
212	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) :=
213	  Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)
214	
215	open Opposite in
216	/-- `dualUnitRingSwapInv` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwap` (`ε ≫ inv ε = 𝟙`). -/
217	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwapInv_comp_dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
218	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
219	    dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwap f W' = 𝟙 _ := by
220	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
221	  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]
222	
223	open Opposite in
224	/-- `dualUnitRingSwap` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwapInv` (`inv ε ≫ ε = 𝟙`). -/
225	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwap_comp_dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
226	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
227	    dualUnitRingSwap f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' = 𝟙 _ := by
228	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
229	  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]
230	
231	open Opposite in
232	/-- The underlying map of `dualUnitRingSwap` is the `.hom` direction of the open-immersion
233	structure-ring isomorphism. -/
234	lemma dualUnitRingSwap_apply {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
235	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)
236	    (x : (X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')) : Type u)) :
237	    (dualUnitRingSwap f W').hom x = (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom x := by
238	  have h := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (dualUnitRingSwap_comp_dualUnitRingSwapInv f W')
239	  have hx := DFunLike.congr_fun h x
240	  change (dualUnitRingSwapInv f W').hom ((dualUnitRingSwap f W').hom x) = x at hx
241	  dsimp [dualUnitRingSwapInv] at hx
242	  have hx' : (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom ((dualUnitRingSwap f W').hom x) = x := by
243	    simpa only [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η] using hx
244	  have hinj : Function.Injective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom :=
245	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv).1
246	  apply hinj
247	  rw [hx']
248	  exact (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom_inv_id x).symm
249	
250	open Opposite in
251	/-- **`invFun` codomain ε is an iso (`.hom` direction).**  The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of
252	`restrictScalars` along `(f.appIso W').hom` (the `.hom`, not `.inv`, of the structure ring iso) is
253	an isomorphism, since `(f.appIso W').hom` is a bijective ring map.  This powers the `invFun`
254	codomain swap (which reindexes the `Over V` section back across `f.opensFunctor` using the
255	`.hom` direction, the mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`'s `.inv`). -/
256	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
257	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
258	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
259	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom)) :=
260	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom
261	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom)
262	
263	open Opposite in
264	/-- **`invFun` codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom (𝟙_Y(W')) ⟶
265	𝟙_X(fW')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom)`,
266	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`.  This is the codomain swap of the reverse
267	transport `invFun` (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`, using the `.hom` direction). -/
268	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapHom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
269	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
270	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom).obj
271	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
272	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')))) :=
273	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom f W'
274	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
275	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom))
276	
277	open Opposite in
278	/-- **ε is an iso for the section-ring relabel** `X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)` (an `eqToHom`-induced,
279	hence bijective, ring map between section rings `𝒪_X(b) → 𝒪_X(a)` for `a = b`).  Phrased at the
280	`X.presheaf` (`CommRingCat`) carrier so `CommRing` is native (`analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
281	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap {X : Scheme.{u}}
282	    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ} (e : a = b) :
283	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
284	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom)) :=
285	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom
286	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)))
287	
288	open Opposite in
289	/-- **Unit-section relabel swap** `restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)) (𝟙_X(b)) ⟶ 𝟙_X(a)`
290	for `a = b` (section opens of `X`).  It is `inv ε` of the relabel ring map, an isomorphism by
291	`isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap`.  This is the `?unit` codomain transport of
292	`sliceDualTransportInv`'s reverse component (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap` for the `he`-relabel). -/
293	noncomputable def unitRelabelSwap {X : Scheme.{u}}
294	    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ} (e : a = b) :
295	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom).obj
296	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj b))) ⟶
297	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj a)) :=
298	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap e
299	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
300	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom))
301	
302	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
303	/-- **Reverse slice transport (the `invFun` of `sliceDualTransport`), extracted top-level.**
304	
305	Given a dual section `ψ : restr V ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V 𝟙_Y` over `Over V`,
306	this produces the X-slice dual section `restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over fV`
307	(`fV = f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`), the mirror of `sliceDualTransport`'s forward `toFun`.
308	
309	For `W'' : (Over fV)ᵒᵖ`, set `P := f⁻¹ᵁ W''.left` (so `f.opensFunctor.obj P = W''.left` only
310	propositionally, via `image_preimage_of_le` since `fV ⊆ range f`).  The component at `W''` is the
311	X-slice mirror of the forward component, conjugated by the `eqToHom`s from `image_preimage_of_le`
312	(mirror of `homLocalSection`):
313	`eqToHom … ≫ (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom).map (ψ.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ≫
314	  dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`,
315	the codomain swap being `dualUnitRingSwapHom = inv (ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom))`
316	(the `.hom`-direction `inv ε`). -/
317	noncomputable def sliceDualTransportInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
318	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ)
319	    (β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
320	    -- β-compatibility (iter-303): `β` is the open-immersion structure ring iso `(f.appIso).inv`,
321	    -- so post-composing it with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the identity on `𝒪_X(f''ᵁP)`.  This is the
322	    -- load-bearing ring identity that collapses the double `restrictScalars` in the reverse
323	    -- component (`?collapse`); it is FALSE for an arbitrary `β`, hence supplied as a hypothesis and
324	    -- discharged at the unique caller (`sliceDualTransport.invFun`) via `Iso.hom_inv_id`.
325	    (hβ : ∀ (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y),
326	        ((β.app (op P)).hom).comp ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom.hom) = RingHom.id _)
327	    (ψ : (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val).dual.obj V : Type u)) :
328	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val.dual).obj V : Type u) := by
329	  refine { app := fun W'' => ?_, naturality := ?_ }
330	  · -- app component at `W''` (over `fV`).  `W' := (unop W'').left ≤ fV`; `P := f⁻¹ᵁ W'`.
331	    -- The down-set facts are established (axiom-clean); the morphism itself is the documented
332	    -- residual below.
333	    set W' := (unop W'').left with hW'
334	    have hW'fV : W' ≤ f ''ᵁ (unop V) := (unop W'').hom.le
335	    have hPV : f ⁻¹ᵁ W' ≤ unop V :=
336	      le_trans ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).monotone hW'fV)
337	        (le_of_eq (f.preimage_image_eq (unop V)))
338	    have he : f ''ᵁ (f ⁻¹ᵁ W') = W' := by
339	      rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
340	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (hW'fV.trans (f.image_le_opensRange (unop V)))
341	    -- **app component — CLOSED axiom-clean (iter-303).**  The X-slice mirror of the forward
342	    -- `toFun`, conjugated across the propositional preimage round-trip `he : f''ᵁ(f⁻¹ᵁ W') = W'`.
343	    -- It is the four-leg composite (all legs concrete):
344	    --   (1) `M.val.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))` : source relabel `M.val(W') ⟶ restr_ρ M.val(fP)`
345	    --       (SEMILINEAR — codomain restricted along `ρ = X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))`,
346	    --       crossing the `𝒪_X(W') ↔ 𝒪_X(fP)` fiber);
347	    --   (2) `restrictScalars ρ |>.map (?collapse ≫ core)` transports the in-fiber-`fP` core:
348	    --       `?collapse` (the double-restrict collapse `M.val(fP) ≅ restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
349	    --       (restrictScalars (β.app P) (M.val fP))` via `restrictScalarsId'App` + `restrictScalarsComp'App`
350	    --       fed the ring identity `hβ (f⁻¹ᵁ W')`), and `core` (legs (3) ψ-reindex `restrictScalars
351	    --       (f.appIso P).hom |>.map (ψ.app …)` + (4) codomain unit swap `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`);
352	    --   (3) `unitRelabelSwap (op he.symm)` : the codomain unit transport `restrictScalars ρ 𝟙_X(fP)
353	    --       ⟶ 𝟙_X(W')` (`inv ε` of the relabel, the new top-level helper).
354	    -- The cross-fiber transport (a single `≫`-chain cannot express it — the relabel is semilinear)
355	    -- is realised by applying the functor `restrictScalars ρ` to the in-fiber-`fP` core.
356	    -- **core (legs 3+4): VERIFIED well-formed in fiber `𝒪_X(fP)` (iter-303).**  The ψ-reindex
357	    -- `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ∘ ψ.app` post-composed with the codomain unit swap
358	    -- `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P` assembles into

542	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
543	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
544	  intro x
545	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
546	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
547	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
548	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩

550	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
551	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
552	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
553	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫

572	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)

577	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
578	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
579	    intro x
580	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
581	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
582	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
583	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
584	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
585	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
586	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
587	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by

610	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`

613	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
614	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
615	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
616	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
617	    M ⟶ N :=
618	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
